textPlain: It would have been cool to read stories about a few more examples of slingshots, as the author calls them.
I think that bootstrapped startups are more likely to be slingshots than rockets. Given lower fundings, but more skin in the game for the founder(s)

Right. Just at the point I expected to find the list, there's only a "Subscribe" button to ignore.Hm. Examples:- Amazon? Long road to profitability, but there was fast early growth.- Space-X, which took a long time to get to a successful launch.- Someone already mentioned Nvidia.- Waymo, which took about 15 years from start to real service, and now is doubling in size more than once a year.- ASML, founded in 1984 and took a long time to become the dominant player in photolithograpy.- Roblox, tiny for over a decade, then gradually found the right market and really grew.Who else.
Fair enough. I definitely should have added nvidia to the list.
either way, I like the term, definitely going to start using that, with the emoji too of course
